_comment: The same fail-closed launcher the recusal repo registers for itself (.claude/settings.json.example), pointed at the plugin's copy of the gate shim. It probes for a python3/python/py that is >=3.9 (macOS/Linux/Windows) and coerces ANY nonzero exit -- missing interpreter, wrong version, `recusal` not pip-installed -- into exit 2, the one BLOCKING hook exit code, so the gate fails CLOSED rather than open. A drift-lock test (tests/test_claude_plugin.py) keeps this command in lockstep with the canonical launcher.

hooks.command: for p in python3 python py; do "$p" -c 'import sys; sys.exit(0 if sys.version_info >= (3, 9) else 1)' 2>/dev/null && { "$p" "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts/recusal_gate.py"; rc=$?; [ "$rc" = 0 ] || { echo 'recusal gate: hook did not run cleanly; failing closed' >&2; exit 2; }; exit 0; }; done; echo 'recusal gate: no working python>=3.9 interpreter; failing closed' >&2; exit 2
